State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 7, 1976

Ms. Ruth Hayes
Administrative Assistant
California School Employees Association
P. O. Box 640
San Jose, CA 95106

Re: Opinion Request
No. 76-069

Dear Ms. Hayes:

Thank you for your letter of May 20, 1976, requesting an
opinion with regard to the California School Employees Asso-
ciation (CSEA) and its Political Action Committee (PACE).
This letter is to advise you that the Commission will issue
an opinion in response to question 1 (a) and (b). The Com-
mission reserves the right, however, to seek additional in-
formation which the Commission or its staff deems relevant to
the preparation of the opinion. A draft opinion will be
prepared by the staff for presentation to the Commission and
a copy of the draft will be forwarded to you as soon as it is
available.

No formal opinion will be issued in response to questions
2 and 3 because the questions pose no substantial question
involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act.
However, I hope the following informal comments will be help-
ful with respect to those questions.

The time spent by CSEA clerical personnel on PACE func-
tions is a nonmonetary contribution by CSEA to PACE. However,
by regulation the Commission has determined that such salary
payments are reportable only if an employee spends more than
ten percent of his or her compensated time in any one month
working on PACE functions. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18423. If PACE reimburses CSEA for the clerical services,
PACE should report the disbursement as an expenditure but
CSEA would not have any reporting obligations by reason of
such reimbursement. Travel and other necessary expenses of
PACE board members should be reported by PACE as expenditures.

Question 4 is too broad to be answered in an opinion of
the Commission, but members of the staff will be glad to
respond to any specific questions you may have. If you do
have any additional questions, please contact Natalie West,
an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:plh

78 089

FOR CONSIDERATION W/E 5/28/76

CALIFORNIA SCHOOL EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION

2350 PARAGON DRIVE      P.O. BOX 640      SAN JOSE, CA 95106      TELEPHONE (408) 263-8000

May 20, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California      95814

.Dear Sir:

The California School Employees Association is a labor organization
qualified for tax exempt status under Section 501(c)(5) of the
Internal Revenue Code.

We are in the process of establishing a political action com-
mittee, open to voluntary membership by members of the Association.
It will be set up as a non-profit, unincorporated organization,
to be known as PACE (Political Action for Classified Employees).
The "membership dues", which will be the primary source of funds,
will come from the regular CSEA dues paid by CSEA members. A
specified amount, $2.50 annually, will be transferred from CSEA
funds to the political action fund in behalf of each member who
chooses to belong to the political organization.

Each member of CSEA will be given the opportunity to decide whether
he wants this portion of his dues allocated for political action.
If the member elects not to have any portion of his dues so
allocated, the $2.50 will simply revert to the CSEA general fund.
In other words, there will be no increase or decrease in the
normal dues of a CSEA member by reason of his election to
participate in PACE. (Refer to Page 24, Section 14, of the
enclosed CSEA Bylaws.)

CSEA's Executive Director, or his designee, will serve on the
Board of Directors as the Secretary/Treasurer and Executive
Director, ex-officio, and any necessary clerical work for the
PACE organization will, initially, be performed by his staff.

The proposed Bylaws of the political action organization (PACE)
are enclosed.

Fair Political Practices Commission                May 20, 1976


We are seeking some clarification as to what the reporting
requirements will be for both the Association (CSEA) and the
political organization (PACE):

1. a) Will the Association (CSEA), by virtue of the dues
       monies transferred annually to the PACE fund, be
       required to file as a Committee under 82013, and
       report the transfer as a "Contribution" to a political
       committee?

   b) Is the transfer reportable by PACE as a lump sum
      contribution by CSEA, or is it treated as individual
      contributions by CSEA members?

2. a) Will time spent by CSEA clerical personnel on PACE
      functions be reportable by CSEA as a contribution?

   b) If the clerical time is reimbursed to CSEA from the
      PACE fund, how is it reported?

3. a) Are travel and other necessary expenses reimbursed
      to PACE Board members from the PACE fund reportable
      in any way?

4. a) Are there any other requirements imposed on CSEA by
      virtue of the establishment of the political action
      arm as proposed in the enclosed Bylaws?


                                        Sincerely,

                                        CALIFORNIA SCHOOL EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION

                                        [signature]
                                        Ruth Hayes
                                        Administrative Assistant

RH/mew
Encls. (2)

BYLAWS

Article I

1. Name

The name of this organization shall be Political Action for Classified Employees, hereinafter referred to as PACE. It is a nonprofit, unincorporated organization comprising the political action arm of the California School Employees Association, hereinafter referred to as CSEA.

2. Purposes

The purposes of PACE are:

a) To educate members, their families, and the community on political issues affecting the welfare of active and retired classified employees of California educational institutions, through study, discussion, development of educational programs, and other appropriate means.

b) To encourage voter registration of members and their families and to ensure maximum voting participation for elections at all levels.

c) To serve as a voluntary funding organization through which members of CSEA may give direct and indirect financial support for such candidates for public office as are deemed worthy from the standpoint of policy issues, without regard to party partisanship, and for statewide ballot measures which are of import to classified employees.

d) To have the status of a political action committee within CSEA subject to the policy and governance structure of CSEA.

Article II

1. Membership

Membership in this organization is open to all active and retired CSEA members, subject to payment of dues as hereinafter prescribed.

-1-

Article III

Board of Directors

1. Composition

There shall be nine Directors of PACE elected from within the nine geographic areas of
CSEA.

2. Eligibility

Any active member of PACE shall be eligible for service as PACE Director, except that if
removed from office for cause as hereinafter prescribed, such person shall be ineligible for
further service.

3. Election

Elections for PACE Director shall be held as follows:

a) The Directors from Areas A, C, E, G, and I shall be elected for a two-year term
in even-numbered years. The Directors from Areas B, D, F, and H shall be elected for a
two-year term in odd-numbered years.

b) Each candidate for PACE Director must:

(1) Be a classified employee of the California public school system or other
educational institution and remain so employed during his/her term of office.

(2) Be a member in good standing of a CSEA chapter within the respective
Area.

(3) Be a dues-paying member of PACE.

(4) File a statement of intent to run for such office with the PACE
Secretary/Treasurer, postmarked not later than June 1 of the year in which the election is to
be held.

The statement of intent shall be in the form of a letter addressed to the PACE
Secretary/Treasurer, which shall contain pertinent information as follows:

-- Candidate's name, mailing address, district of employment, CSEA Chapter
No., Area designation.

-- Brief statement of reasons why candidate feels he or she is qualified for
the position.

-2-

c)    The PACE Secretary/Treasurer shall then cause an announcement to be issued
certifying all candidates filing statements of intent and listing their qualifications.

d)    The PACE Secretary/Treasurer shall cause a ballot to be prepared containing
the names of all eligible candidates, which shall be forwarded to each chapter within the
respective Area, postmarked not later than September 15 of the election year.

e)    Upon receipt of the ballot, the Chapter President shall announce to the chapter
membership the date, place, and time of the election meeting, which shall be scheduled
between November 1 and November 15.

f)    Only active, dues-paying members of PACE within the chapter shall be eligible
to vote at such meeting.

g)    The PACE Secretary/Treasurer shall forward an accounting of chapter members
belonging to PACE to the Chapter President by September 15.

h)    Each chapter shall be entitled to cast one vote for each 100 PACE members, or
fraction thereof. The vote shall be determined by a plurality of the qualified PACE
membership present and voting at the meeting.

i)    The ballot shall then be completed and certified by the signature of the
Chapter Secretary and forwarded to the PACE Secretary/Treasurer, postmarked not later than
midnight, November 15.

j)    The PACE Secretary/Treasurer shall tabulate the ballots and forward the results
to the PACE Chairperson, who shall cause the matter to be placed on the Agenda of the next
regular or special meeting of the PACE Board.

k)    The PACE Board, at its next meeting, shall first audit any ballots declared
invalid by the Secretary/Treasurer, and shall sustain or reverse the invalidation. It shall then
audit all ballots, tabulate the ballots, and certify the successful candidates. The PACE
Chairperson shall notify all candidates of the results, and arrange for publication of same in
The School Employee as soon thereafter as possible.

l)    In the event of a tie between two or more candidates, the PACE Secretary/
Treasurer shall immediately notify all concerned chapter presidents and candidates and shall
provide each chapter with a new ballot listing only the tied candidates. The Chapter President
shall then call a special meeting to again cast its ballot, and the rules governing the run-off shall
be the same as for the original election, except the ballot shall be returned to the PACE
Secretary/Treasurer not later than December 15. If a tie exists at the conclusion of a run-off,
the election shall be determined by lot by the PACE Board.

m)    Successful candidates shall take office on January 1 of the succeeding year.

n)    In the event only one candidate files a statement of intent, the PACE
Secretary/Treasurer shall certify that candidate as the elected PACE Director and so inform
the PACE Chairperson.


-3-

4. Interim Board

a) Notwithstanding the above Section 3, the CSEA Board of Directors shall serve as the interim PACE Board from August 7, 1976 through December 31, 1976.

b) Nominations for all nine PACE Directorships will be solicited and accepted immediately following the 1976 Annual Conference, with elections being held in November 1976.

c) Directors for Areas A, C, E, G, and I will be elected for a two-year term, January 1, 1977 through December 31, 1978; and Directors for Areas B, D, F, and H will be elected for a one-year term, January 1, 1977 through December 31, 1977. Thereafter, elections shall be carried out in the manner specified in Section 3.

d) This section shall have no force or effect after December 31, 1978.

5. Electioneering Ethics

All candidates for PACE Director shall be subject to CSEA Policy 204 regarding campaign spending and electioneering practices.

6. Vacancies

a) Vacancies in the office of PACE Director may be declared to exist for inability to serve, or for cause, such as excessive absence, failure to adhere to established policies and procedures, or conduct detrimental to the purposes of CSEA or the PACE organization.

Declaration of such vacancy shall be made by the PACE Board of Directors, but may be appealed to the CSEA Board of Directors under regulations prescribed by that body.

b) All vacancies declared to exist shall be filled as soon as possible in the normal election process to fill the unexpired term only.

7. Meetings

The PACE Board of Directors shall meet at least quarterly, but as often as necessary to conduct essential business.

8. Parliamentary Authority

Where not otherwise prescribed within these PACE Bylaws, the latest edition of "Robert's Rules of Order" shall be the authority for the conduct of all meetings of PACE.

-4-

9.    Expenses

Expenses of individual PACE Board members for attendance at meetings and other
official PACE activities shall be reimbursed from PACE funds, and on the same basis as
contained in CSEA Policies 114 and 115.


Article IV

Officers

1.    General

All PACE Directors shall be considered as Officers. The PACE Executive Officers shall be
Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Secretary/Treasurer, and PACE Executive Director. All
Executive Officers shall perform their duties under the on-going control of the PACE Board of
Directors.

2.    Chairperson

a)    The Chairperson shall be elected by and from the members of the PACE Board of
Directors at the first meeting of each year, and shall serve until the immediate successor is
elected.

b)    In the event of a vacancy during a term in the office of Chairperson, the
Vice-Chairperson shall become the Chairperson.

c)    The Chairperson shall preside over all meetings of the PACE Board of Directors.

3.    Vice-Chairperson

a)    The Vice-Chairperson shall be elected by and from the members of the PACE Board
of Directors at the first meeting of each year, or at the next meeting following a vacancy in the
office of Vice-Chairperson, whichever occurs first, and shall serve until the immediate
successor is elected.

b)    The Vice-Chairperson shall serve as an assistant to the Chairperson, and shall
perform such duties as are assigned consistent with the office.

4.    Secretary/Treasurer

The PACE Executive Director shall serve as Secretary/Treasurer for PACE. The
Secretary/Treasurer shall have custody over the official minutes of all meetings of PACE and
shall be responsible for carrying on the necessary and appropriate correspondence on behalf of
PACE. The Secretary/Treasurer shall be responsible for the maintenance of all funds, keeping

-5-

records of receipts and expenditures, filing such reports as are required under the Fair Political
Practices Act, and shall render such reports as may be required by the PACE Board of
Directors.

5. PACE Executive Director

The CSEA Executive Director or his designee shall serve ex-officio as the PACE Executive
Director. The PACE Executive Director shall be the chief staff officer for PACE, and shall
maintain supervision and control over other paid staff as are necessary to carry out the
activities of PACE.

Article V

Employment of Staff

1. Employment Authority

The CSEA Board of Directors shall be the employment authority for all paid staff of
PACE, including the setting of the terms and conditions of employment and establishment of
job descriptions within the overall personnel policies of CSEA.

Salaries and expenses of such staff shall be paid from the PACE fund, including that
portion of the PACE Executive Director's salary and expenses which are determined to be
apportioned to PACE activities on a pro-rata basis. ·

Article VI

1. Dues

a) PACE membership dues shall be as follows:
Active $2.50 per year
Retired $2.00 per year

b) Active members shall contribute via payroll deduction of dues as prescribed in the
CSEA Bylaws, Article VII, Section 14.

c) Retired members shall contribute annually in advance.

2. Contributions

Contributions, gifts, and bequests may be accepted for the PACE political action fund by
the PACE Board at any time when they are without restriction as to use, and in compliance
with the Fair Political Practices Act and Federal law.

-6-

3.    Fiscal Year

The PACE fiscal year shall be January 1 through December 31.

4.    Budget

a)    The development and proposal of the PACE Budget shall be the responsibility of the PACE Board of Directors, and shall be submitted to the CSEA Board of Directors for approval no later than October 1 of each year.

b)    The PACE Board of Directors shall be responsible for the control and expenditure of funds, subject to guidelines and limitations as contained in the Standing Rules.

c)    An annual budget report shall be published and made available to the PACE membership no later than March 15 of each year in a manner prescribed by the PACE Board.

Article VII

1.    Bylaws Revision

Amendments to these PACE Bylaws may be made as follows:

a)    By a two-thirds vote, the PACE Board of Directors may make recommendation for revision to the CSEA Board of Directors.

b)    If the CSEA Board of Directors, by a two-thirds vote, approves such revision, it shall then be forwarded to Chapter Presidents for ratification by PACE members at a special meeting. The PACE Secretary/Treasurer shall prepare ballots and voting guidelines, with the vote being cast as per requirements specified in Article III, Sections (i) and (h). It shall require two-thirds of the total votes cast for ratification.

c)    Amendments shall become effective at the time of ratification, unless otherwise specified at the time of amendment.

2.    Revisions to Standing Rules

a)    The PACE Board of Directors may, by two-thirds vote, submit recommendations for adoption or revision of Standing Rules where deemed necessary.

b)    The CSEA Board of Directors shall have the authority, by two-thirds vote, to adopt such recommendations, provided that such Standing Rules do not conflict with the scope of authority granted the PACE Board by these Bylaws.

c)    All such Standing Rules so adopted shall be printed in the next edition of The School Employee where printing deadline requirements can be met.

-7-

PACE STANDING RULES

1.    Political Action Training Program

     The PACE Board of Directors shall develop a political action training course for PACE
members which shall be conducted by PACE in accordance with procedures to be developed
by the PACE Board.

     a)    The purpose of the program will be to educate members in the political
processes applicable to all parties and not to advocate any partisan philosophy or viewpoint.

     b)    The content of the training program shall include, but not be limited to, the
theory and practical application of the principles, methods and skills of actually conducting
effective political campaigns.

     c)    The instruction in the training program shall be provided by PACE Directors or
other competent instructors, and may include persons who have been elected to public office.

     d)    The cost of maintaining the political action training program shall be a charge
against the PACE budget.


2.    Expenditures

     The PACE Board of Directors shall be the sole-authority for approving expenditures from
the PACE budget. All expenditures shall be made by check, signed by both the Chairperson
and Treasurer.


3.    Allocations

     PACE financial support of candidates, campaigns and ballot measures shall be made
within the guidelines set forth below:

     a)    Maximum campaign funding limitations are:

               Statewide ballot measures/referendums                              $20,000
               Statewide candidates                                              $10,000
               Legislative District candidates                                   $ 5,000
               School Board candidates                                           $ 1,000
               Per fund-raising function                                         $ 2,000

     b)    Procedures for providing local endorsements of district candidates considering
input from local PACE members, shall be developed by the PACE Board. All such local
endorsement activities shall be under the direction of the PACE Director for the Area.

     c)    Application forms for PACE financial support will be developed by PACE and
made available to local chapters. Requests for funding shall only be made after following the
above approved endorsement processes.


-8-

d) Final decisions on funding allocations shall rest with the PACE Board, and shall
be based on:

(1) local endorsement recommendations
(2) voting record of candidate
(3) public statements of candidate
(4) results of questionnaires sent candidate
(5) PACE resources and budget needs

e) Approved PACE financial support shall be issued in check form, and presented
to the recipient by PACE members.

f) To comply with state and federal allocations reporting requirements, presen-
tation information is required. The members who present the PACE check shall provide PACE
the needed reporting information on forms provided by PACE.

4. Survey

The PACE Board shall conduct an annual survey of PACE members to determine the
general needs and legislative issues the membership feels should be addressed by the PACE
Board.

-9-

If you do have any additional questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Box 640 San Jose, CA 95106 Re: Opinion Request No. 76-069 Dear Ms. Hayes: Thank you for your letter of May 20, 1976, requesting an opinion with regard to the California School Employees Association (CSEA) and its Political Action Committee (PACE). This letter is to advise you that the Commission will issue an opinion in response to question 1 (a) and (b). The Commission reserves the right, however, to seek additional information which the Commission or its staff deems relevant to the preparation of the opinion. A draft opinion will be prepared by the staff for presentation to the Commission and a copy of the draft will be forwarded to you as soon as it is available. No formal opinion will be issued in response to questions 2 and 3 because the questions pose no substantial question involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful with respect to those questions. The time spent by CSEA clerical personnel on PACE functions is a non-monetary contribution by CSEA to PACE. However, by regulation the Commission has determined that such salary payments are reportable only if an employee spends more than ten percent of his or her compensated time in any one month working on PACE functions. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18423. If PACE reimburses CSEA for the clerical services, PACE should report the disbursement as an expenditure but CSEA would not have any reporting obligations by reason of such reimbursement. Travel and other necessary expenses of PACE Board members should be reported by PACE as expenditures. Question 4 is too broad to be answered in an opinion of the Commission, but members of the staff will be glad to respond to any specific questions you may have. If you do have any additional questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.
